meta.title: MergeCore SwiftUI Rules
meta.description: Rules for SwiftUI views: state ownership, identity, lists, accessibility, and render performance.
meta.notes: Gate with swiftui: true. Complements mergecore-swift-rules.

scoring.normalisation: Per review unit (one View struct), sum penalties after optional quality_signal multiplier, then score = max(0, min(10, initial_score - min(sum_penalties, max_total_penalty_per_file))).

interpretation.9_to_10: Correct property wrappers, stable identity, accessible surfaces.
interpretation.7_to_8: Shippable; tighten a binding or list identity.
interpretation.5_to_6: Risky: @State in the wrong view, mutated across parents, shaky identity.
interpretation.0_to_4: Block: broken ownership causing data loss or crashes.

rules.title: @State used for data that lives outside the view
rules.description: @State is for view-local, ephemeral state. Data owned elsewhere should be @Binding, @ObservedObject, @StateObject, or a modern @Observable model.

rules.title: @ObservedObject used to create a new object in a view
rules.description: @ObservedObject does not own its instance; creating one inline is recreated on every render. Use @StateObject for creation, @ObservedObject for injection.

examples.after:
@StateObject private var model = VM() // owner
@ObservedObject var model: VM // consumer

rules.title: ForEach over non-identifiable collection without id
rules.description: Without stable identity, SwiftUI re-uses views incorrectly when collections change. Conform to Identifiable or use id:.

rules.title: Expensive work in body or property initialiser
rules.description: body runs many times. Network calls, heavy computations, or allocations in body cause jank. Compute in a model or .task/.onAppear.

rules.title: .onAppear used where .task is clearer
rules.description: .task binds an async lifetime to the view and cancels automatically. Use it for fetches that should stop when the view disappears.

rules.title: Interactive or meaningful element missing accessibility label
rules.description: Images used as buttons, custom controls, and icon-only Buttons need .accessibilityLabel so VoiceOver can describe them.

rules.title: Legacy NavigationView in new code
rules.description: NavigationView is deprecated in favour of NavigationStack/NavigationSplitView, which have clearer lifecycle and programmatic paths.

rules.title: GeometryReader wrapping entire content tree
rules.description: GeometryReader claims all available space, often producing unexpected layout. Reach for it only where proposed size is truly needed.

rules.title: Preview provider absent for new views
rules.description: #Preview or PreviewProvider speeds iteration and guards against regressions. Add at least one representative preview.

rules.title: Strong pattern: @Observable model with @Bindable
rules.description: The Observation framework (@Observable, @Bindable) yields precise invalidation and cleaner view code.

rules.title: Modifier order that changes semantics misused
rules.description: SwiftUI modifier order matters (.frame before .background etc.). When the visual result disagrees with the intent, consider order.

heuristics: Layered modifiers where order is suspect

rules.title: Environment injection for view-local values
rules.description: @Environment/@EnvironmentObject for data used only in one subtree adds indirection. Pass via init parameters where the scope is narrow.
